POS OF: REPLY ISO ITS MTN FOR SUMM JUDGMENT; EVIDENTIARY OBJECITONS; [PROPOSED] ORDER; [PROPOSED] ORDER FILED BY DEFENDANT WILLOWS INN BED AND BREAKFAST

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLOWS INN BED AND BREAKFAST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO MAINTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF PRODUCTION. THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS VIABLE BECAUSE DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT IT HAD NO CONTROL OVER THE PARKING LOT. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT IT DID NOT OWE A DUTY OF CARE TO PLAINTIFF. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT REGARDING BREACH OF THE DUTY AND CAUSATION. DEFENDANT PROMISED THAT THE PARKING LOT HAD A GUARD AND WOULD BE SECURED WHEN THE GUARD WAS NOT PRESENT, BUT THAT DID NOT OCCUR. THE COURT DENIES SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON THE BREACH OF ORAL CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER THE PERSON MR. BERNSTEIN COMMUNICATED WITH HAD AUTHORITY TO ENTER INTO A CONTRACT. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT THE CONTRACT LACKED CONSIDERATION. MR. BERNSTEIN PAID A $20 FEE TO DEFENDANT. IN ADDITION, "[I]T IS WELL ESTABLISHED THAT A CONSIDERATION FOR A CONTRACT IS EQUALLY VALUABLE WHETHER IT MOVE TO THE OTHER PARTY BOUND OR TO A THIRD PARTY. CONSIDERATION DOES NOT HAVE TO MOVE TO A PROMISOR." CECHETTINI V. CONSUMER ASSOC. LTD. (1968) 260 CAL.APP.2D 295, 298. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-23-2014 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Aug-05-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adj
Off Calendar May-08-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. The Court adopted the tentative ruling. DEFENDANT W-PARKING, LLC's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is off calendar per the April 24, 2014 dismissal. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-23-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER CAPALBO, ESQ., 619.525.7626, FOR PLAINTIFF ESSENTIA INSURANCE.
JOHN WILCOXSON, ESQ., 949.228.5898, FOR DEFENDANT W-PARKING, LLC.
RAYMOND TITTMANN, ESQ., 510.628.2192, AND JODI SWICK, ESQ., 510.628.2194, FOR DEFENDANT WILLOWS INN BED AND BREAKFAST.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT WILLOWS INN BED AND BREAKFAST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication IS DENIED. Defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The negligence cause of action is viable because defendant fails to show that it had no control over the parking lot. Defendant fails to show that it did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff. There is a triable issue of fact regarding breach of the duty and causation. Defendant promised that the parking lot had a guard and would be secured when the guard was not present, but that did not occur. The Court denies summary adjudication on the breach of oral contract cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact whether the person Mr. Bernstein communicated with had authority to enter into a contract. Defendant fails to show that the contract lacked consideration. Mr. Bernstein paid a $20 fee to defendant. In addition, "[i]t is well established that a consideration for a contract is equally valuable whether it move to the other party bound or to a third party. Consideration does not have to move to a promisor." Cechettini v. Consumer Assoc. Ltd. (1968) 260 Cal.App.2d 295, 298. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-26-2013 Continued To Jun-09-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-25-2013 Set For Trial On Jan-06-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
